everyone.name: Carla Gaggini
everyone.bio: Carla has been managing events and communities since 2011. She started with experimental music festivals and eventually ended up in Tech, where she fell in love with its ecosystem. During her career she has produced and run many conferences (yes, also the virtual ones!), meetups, webinars and hackathons. Formerly at Container Solutions managing everything "WTF is Cloud Native" and "Software Circus", she currently buzzes around Cilium and eBPF at Isovalent. She is also one of the organisers of KCD UK, a CNCF and OpenUK Ambassador, and the 2022 CNCF Marketing Committee Chair. Carla drinks way too much tea (mainly green), has an unhealthy obsession for Japan and a big passion for art, music, cinema, philosophy and fashion. Recent blogs: https://blog.container-solutions.com/why-your-event-needs-a-code-of-conduct-and-you-need-to-enforce-it https://blog.container-solutions.com/how-hard-is-it-to-deliver-a-virtual-conference https://blog.container-solutions.com/tech-events-in-the-time-of-covid-19-5-tips-to-surviveWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: This is an invaluable opportunity to support the CNCF and the Cloud Native community. I'm incredibly excited to help new joiners and spread more awareness around all the initiatives they can embrace within the CNCF, the various KCDs and the Cloud ecosystem in general.

everyone.name: Thomas Labarussias
everyone.bio: I'm OSS/Ecosystem Advocate at Sysdig, the company which created and open-sourced Falco, the Security Runtime Engine for Kubernetes and Cloud-Native technologies. I worked for Qonto, a modern banking for SMEs and freelancers, where I managed their Kubernetes clusters and the enthusiastic tools around, like ArgoCD, Traefik, Prometheus. I also assisted for many years pure-players and e-business companies for a large managed service provider, as an AWS expert and FinOps. I'm one of the longest tenured members of the Falco community, and the creator of Falcosidekick and Falcosidekick-UI, two major components of the Falco ecosystem.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: As an SRE for several years, I've been able to use a number of CNCF projects in production. I'm also one of the maintainers of the most advanced OSS security project in the landscape. I would like to help even more architects and users to integrate these projects into their infrastructures. Moreover, in 2024, the KubeCon EU will be in Paris, France, it's important for me to do my best to help the organizers and show the vitality of French tech.

everyone.name: Rafael Brito
everyone.bio: Rafael "Rafa" Brito has over 30 years of industry experience in distributed computing, Kubernetes, Cloud, grid/HPC, and high-frequency trading infrastructure (HFT). Rafa is a principal engineer at StormForge by CloudBolt, dedicated to delivering Kubernetes optimization and scalability to customers. Before joining StormForge, Rafa worked at VMware, Citigroup, and NYSE. Rafa co-organizes the KCD Texas and CNCF Austin Community Group, and he serves as co-chair of CNCF TAG Operational Resilience (as of 2025-2026). Rafa is the co-author of the best-selling book "Acing the CKA Exam" 2nd Edition. When not working with Kubernetes, you'll find Rafa training for marathons at the Lady Bird Trail in Austin, TX, and fanatically rooting for his Brazilian soccer team, Flamengo.

everyone.name: Beatrice Forslund
everyone.bio: DevOps Engineer. Interested in Kubernetes, as well as GitOps tools such as ArgoCD.

everyone.name: Amit Kumar Jain
everyone.bio: I specialise in cloud‑native technologies across the CNCF landscape - Kubernetes, DevOps automation, distributed systems, and modern platform engineering. I love challenging myself with advanced technologies and implementing complex automation.

everyone.name: Marcus Antonio De Souza Costa
everyone.bio: I am a technology professional with strong experience in infrastructure, networking, and DevOps, specializing in containerized and cloud-native environments. I work extensively with CNCF technologies such as Kubernetes and Docker, focusing on CI/CD automation, platform reliability, and DevSecOps practices. My background combines hands-on engineering with academic research in automated security mechanisms and traffic analysis. I am particularly interested in securing microservices platforms through observability, policy enforcement, and resilient architecture design.

ambassadors.name: Carla Gaggini
ambassadors.bio: Carla has been managing events and communities since 2011. She started with experimental music festivals and eventually ended up in Tech, where she fell in love with its ecosystem. During her career she has produced and run many conferences (yes, also the virtual ones!), meetups, webinars and hackathons. Formerly at Container Solutions managing everything "WTF is Cloud Native" and "Software Circus", she currently buzzes around Cilium and eBPF at Isovalent. She is also one of the organisers of KCD UK, a CNCF and OpenUK Ambassador, and the 2022 CNCF Marketing Committee Chair. Carla drinks way too much tea (mainly green), has an unhealthy obsession for Japan and a big passion for art, music, cinema, philosophy and fashion. Recent blogs: https://blog.container-solutions.com/why-your-event-needs-a-code-of-conduct-and-you-need-to-enforce-it https://blog.container-solutions.com/how-hard-is-it-to-deliver-a-virtual-conference https://blog.container-solutions.com/tech-events-in-the-time-of-covid-19-5-tips-to-surviveWhy I am excited to be an ambassador: This is an invaluable opportunity to support the CNCF and the Cloud Native community. I'm incredibly excited to help new joiners and spread more awareness around all the initiatives they can embrace within the CNCF, the various KCDs and the Cloud ecosystem in general.

ambassadors.name: Junya Okabe
ambassadors.bio: He is passionate about cloud native technologies, especially Kubernetes, and is a professional in this field. He leads the localization of Kubernetes and CNCF documentation as a localization approver in SIG-Docs and a reviewer for several projects. Additionally, he is an active OSS contributor to Kubernetes and related projects. To encourage more people to join the community, he is part of the organizer team for Kubernetes Upstream Training. (Related blog post: https://kubernetes.io/blog/2024/10/28/k8s-upstream-training-japan-spotlight/ )

ambassadors.name: Di Xu
ambassadors.bio: Di Xu is passionate about open source projects and get involved in. He is a top-60 code contributor in Kubernetes community and founds CNCF Sandbox project Clusternet. He is also an avid speaker and has spoken many times at open source conferences and meetups, including KubeCon, Open Source Summit, etc. Nowadays he is specialized in cloud native, Kubernetes and infrastructure.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: I've been an active member of the Kubernetes community since 2016. I am eager to become a CNCF Ambassador to give back to the open source community and help drive cloud native adoption. My practical experience with CNCF technologies can provide valuable insights to users. As an ambassador, I look forward to mentoring new contributors, staying connected to the latest developments, speaking at events, creating tutorials, and connecting with the cloud native community. This role aligns well with my passion for open source software and interest in emerging cloud native technologies. I am excited by the opportunity to contribute meaningfully as an ambassador, share my knowledge with others and help advance the CNCF ecosystem.

ambassadors.name: Michael Yao
ambassadors.bio: I'm an active maintainer to kubernetes, Istio, OTel, and more website, a speaker in KubeCon and KCD, and a frequent member of KubeCon Program Committee. I own a technical website about cloud native and AI, and write those content in https://docs.daocloud.io/en/

ambassadors.name: Thomas Vitale
ambassadors.bio: Thomas Vitale is a software engineer with a focus on building cloud native solutions, currently working at Systematic in Denmark. He is the author of the "Cloud Native Spring in Action" book and plays an active role in the cloud native ecosystem as a CNCF Ambassador, Co-Chair of the CNCF Application Development Working Group, Oracle ACE Pro, and Testcontainers Community Champion.A strong advocate of open source collaboration, Thomas contributes to various projects in the Java and cloud-native space, including Spring AI, Testcontainers, Carvel, Dapr, and Buildpacks. He is also passionate about sharing his expertise with the community through talks, workshops, and writing on technical topics.

ambassadors.name: Kaslin Fields
ambassadors.bio: Kaslin Fields is a Developer Advocate at Google Cloud, CNCF Ambassador, and a contributor to Open Source Kubernetes. She is passionate about making Cloud Native technology exciting and fun to learn for everyone from seasoned pros to total newcomers. In addition to her work supporting the development of cloud native technologies as a contributor, she also aims to spread the word about this awesome field by producing talks, videos, blogs, documentation, comics, and more.Why I am excited to be an ambassador: The Cloud Native ecosystem is active and growing, which can make it difficult for community members building awesome things to get the support they need. As an ambassador, I am passionate about providing support to folks building their own Cloud Native communities, technologies, and more.

ambassadors.name: Divine Akachukwu Odazie
ambassadors.bio: The Founder of EverythingDevOps, Divine Odazie, is a Developer Relations Professional and Technical Writer with over 8+ years of experience in Technology and a track record in Backend Engineering, DevOps, Cloud Native, and Developer Relations on a global scale. He has given talks/workshops at developer conferences like Open Source Summit Europe, KubeCon North America, Cloud Native Rejekts, and Ansible Contributor Summit.  He also supports the cloud native community in Africa as a KCD (Kubernetes Community Days) Organizer, and other communities empowering Africa with Technology.

ambassadors.name: Ankit Kumar
ambassadors.bio: Ankit is a cloud engineer and developer relations expert with extensive experience in AI and cloud infrastructure, having worked with Fine AI, Xpander AI, Qulifire AI, Myrror Security, and SkyPurple Cloud. A former LFX mentee, CNCF contributor, and Approver in the CNCF Glossary, he has played a key role in open-source ecosystems, driving innovation in cloud-native technologies, orchestration, and microservices. With a deep passion for bridging technology and community, Ankit excels in fostering adoption, delivering impactful technical content, and advocating for scalable AI and cloud solutions.

kubestronauts.name: Beatrice Forslund
kubestronauts.bio: DevOps Engineer. Interested in Kubernetes, as well as GitOps tools such as ArgoCD.

kubestronauts.name: Dogukan Turan
kubestronauts.bio: Dogukan Turan is a DevOps Engineer working with Kubernetes and cloud-native technologies. In his professional life he focuses on running and maintaining Kubernetes environments and working with tools from the cloud-native ecosystem.  A long-time Linux enthusiast, he enjoys staying close to the system, exploring how things work under the hood, and understanding the internals of the tools he uses. Much of his interest revolves around Kubernetes and the broader CNCF landscape.  He is also one of the organizers of DevOpsDays Istanbul and DevOps Türkiye.  I

kubestronauts.name: D S Charan Avula
kubestronauts.bio: I am a Cloud Solutions Architect and Kubernetes professional with 11+ years of experience designing, securing, and operating enterprise-scale cloud-native platforms across Azure and AWS. I specialize in Kubernetes security, DevSecOps, and GitOps, with hands-on expertise in CNCF technologies including Kubernetes, Argo CD, and cloud-native security practices. As a Kubestronaut, I am passionate about building secure, scalable, and cost-efficient platforms aligned with Zero Trust, NIST, and CIS standards. I enjoy contributing to the cloud-native community by sharing practical insights and continuously learning emerging technologies.

kubestronauts.name: Krzysztof Wachowicz
kubestronauts.bio: DevOps | Linux Engineer with over 14 years of experience in designing, deploying, and automating cloud and infrastructure solutions. My goal is to deliver stable, scalable, and secure infrastructure solutions that streamline developer workflows and minimize operational risk.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Ana Pedra
goldenKubestronauts.bio: Cloud DevOps Engineer | Golden Kubestronaut 🚀 | Hackathon winner 🏆 and lifelong learner with 40+ certifications across AWS, Azure, Google Cloud, Red Hat, and Kubernetes. Passionate about cloud, Kubernetes, security, and automation — always learning, building, and giving back to the community.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Julius Wolf
goldenKubestronauts.bio: DevOps Engineer based in Germany. With passion for Cloud, Kubernetes und cloud-native technologies.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Yevhenii Kurasov
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I am a Site Reliability Engineer focused on designing resilient, secure, and compliant cloud-native systems. I combine software engineering, automation, and observability practices to solve complex reliability problems in production environments. I enjoy exploring new technologies, learning modern tools, and applying them to improve performance, reliability, and developer experience.

goldenKubestronauts.name: Roman Stein
goldenKubestronauts.bio: I'm a Site Reliability Engineer with a strong foundation in software development and system architecture, focused on building and operating reliable, highly available systems across public and private cloud infrastructure. My work involves designing and managing Kubernetes clusters, enabling GitOps-based workflows for infrastructure and application deployments, and driving automation through Infrastructure as Code practices. I’m passionate about creating resilient, scalable platforms that empower development teams and ensure operational excellence.

maintainers.name: Xing Yang
maintainers.bio:
Kubernetes Storage SIG co-chair, 
CNCF Storage TAG co-chair
